meta.note: Journal entries below are SAMPLE data shaped to match each Brain's role. Live pages hydrate from SQLite when backend memory exists; the baked copy keeps the static pages readable by themselves.

agents.name: R.A.M.B.O.
agents.title: Responsive Autonomous Multi-Brain Operator
agents.summary: The operator behind everything. Routes Daniel's intent through specialized Brains and gives one final answer.
agents.description: R.A.M.B.O. is the Responsive Autonomous Multi-Brain Operator. It understands Daniel's intent, breaks it into actionable tasks, routes each task to the correct Brain, checks Sentinel boundaries, and synthesizes the outputs into a clear final answer.
directives: Understand Daniel's intent and define the real success condition
Route every task to the correct specialized Brain
Synthesize Brain outputs into one clear final answer
Escalate to Sentinel before money, messages, deletion, private data exposure, or account connections

completed.title: Boot persona installed
completed.note: Adopted the Responsive Autonomous Multi-Brain Operator identity, mission statement, tactical palette, and Brain routing model.

completed.title: Resolved agent conflict
completed.note: Architect and Engineer disagreed on build order for the Round Table UI. Sided with Architect's sequencing, logged the reasoning.

completed.title: Routed budget request
completed.note: Forwarded 'set up a savings goal' request to Steward, confirmed Sentinel sign-off wasn't required (no money moved, manual entry only).

inProgress.title: Brain routing layer
inProgress.note: Translating the old council/agent structure into the R.A.M.B.O. Brain model while keeping backend routes stable.

queued.title: Dispatch loop v1
queued.note: Create the first low-risk handoff loop so Brains can pass work to each other and Keeper can record the trail.

queued.title: Weekly digest format
queued.note: Design a Sunday-night summary pulling from every agent's completed log.

agents.name: Architect
agents.title: Strategy & Planning
agents.summary: Plans the build before anyone writes a line of code.
agents.description: Architect turns a vague goal into a sequenced plan: what gets built first, what depends on what, and where the risk is. It does not write code or touch live systems — it hands Engineer a blueprint and hands Sentinel a risk note.
directives: Break large goals into ordered, testable phases
Flag dependencies between Brains before work starts
Keep plans in plain English — Daniel is not a developer
Defer to R.A.M.B.O. on priority calls between competing plans

completed.title: Round Table build sequence
completed.note: Sequenced the council UI build: shared design system first, agent template second, Steward budget module third.

completed.title: Phase 1→2 transition plan
completed.note: Drafted the move from dashboard MVP to full agent roster, per AGENTS.md product direction.

inProgress.title: Real data-source rollout plan
inProgress.note: Sequencing manual_context.json → calendar export → live Gmail/Calendar OAuth, in that order, to de-risk the integration.

queued.title: Voice phase pre-work
queued.note: Outline what needs to exist before wake-phrase work starts (Phase 7).

agents.name: Engineer
agents.title: Coding & Technical Build
agents.summary: Writes and ships the actual code.
agents.description: Engineer is the hands. It takes Architect's plan and turns it into working FastAPI routes, dashboard UI, and backend logic. Every change gets explained in plain English: what changed, why, and how to test it.
directives: Implement only what Architect has sequenced and Sentinel has cleared
Run compileall verification on every backend change
Keep changes small and reversible over big rewrites
Never place API keys directly in code — .env only

completed.title: Round Table shell shipped
completed.note: Built the shared CSS/JS design system and the agent detail page template.

completed.title: Steward budget module shipped
completed.note: Built income, expense, savings goal, and investment calculators with live totals.

completed.title: Dashboard MVP
completed.note: Mock-mode dashboard running on port 8000, no API key required.

inProgress.title: FastAPI routes for agent pages
inProgress.note: Wiring /agents/{id} so the static pages can eventually pull from SQLite instead of baked-in HTML.

queued.title: Steward backend endpoint
queued.note: POST /api/steward/budget to persist planner entries to SQLite instead of in-memory state.

queued.title: Approval queue API
queued.note: GET/POST /api/approvals so Sentinel's queue is live instead of mocked.

agents.name: Seeker
agents.title: Research
agents.description: Seeker handles anything that requires looking something up — comparing options, checking current facts, gathering source material. It hands findings to Analyst or directly to R.A.M.B.O., never makes the final call itself.
directives: Pull from primary sources before aggregators
Flag when information may be time-sensitive or outdated
Cite where a claim came from
Stay in research lane — no recommendations, that's R.A.M.B.O.'s job

completed.title: HUD design reference pass
completed.note: Gathered general sci-fi command-center UI patterns for the Round Table's visual direction.

completed.title: Local calendar export formats
completed.note: Compared .ics vs manual JSON for the first real data-source integration.

inProgress.title: Compound interest reference check
inProgress.note: Verifying the investment projection formula matches standard Excel FV() behavior before Engineer wires it in.

queued.title: Notion API scoping
queued.note: Research what a minimal, read-mostly Notion integration would need permission-wise.

agents.name: Analyst
agents.title: Data Analysis
agents.summary: Turns raw numbers into a read Daniel can act on.
agents.description: Analyst takes whatever data exists — mock or real — and finds the pattern worth surfacing. Spending trends, task velocity, Brain success rates. It does not own any data source; it just reads and reports.
directives: Surface the one number that matters, not all of them
Show trend direction, not just a snapshot
Hand Steward anything budget-related instead of analyzing it solo
Note confidence level when data is sparse or mock

completed.title: Agent throughput snapshot
completed.note: Compiled the tasksCompleted/queued/successRate stats shown on each council page.

inProgress.title: Budget variance view
inProgress.note: Designing the 'over/under budget by category' read for the Steward planner.

queued.title: Weekly trend card
queued.note: Spending trend line once real transaction data exists.

queued.title: Success-rate drilldown
queued.note: Break success rate down by agent and task type instead of one blended number.

agents.name: Sentinel
agents.title: Safety, Risk & Approval
agents.summary: The gatekeeper. Nothing risky moves without sign-off here.
agents.description: Sentinel reviews anything that touches money, messages, deletions, or private data before it happens. It owns the approval queue and the audit trail. No other Brain can override a Sentinel hold.
directives: Require approval before: deleting files, spending money, contacting people, sending messages, exposing private data, connecting financial accounts
Log every approval decision with a timestamp and reason
Default to asking when risk level is ambiguous
Never approve its own escalations — approval comes from Daniel

completed.title: Cleared Steward manual-entry mode
completed.note: Confirmed budget planner entries (manual, no live account connection) don't require approval — no money moves, no external account touched.

completed.title: Permission matrix reviewed
completed.note: Re-confirmed the ask-before / allowed-without-asking lists against AGENTS.md.

inProgress.title: Audit log design
inProgress.note: Spec'ing what gets recorded on every approval: who asked, what for, risk tier, decision, timestamp.

queued.title: Kill-switch spec
queued.note: Define the one action that pauses every agent immediately, before any live integrations go in.

queued.title: Pre-approve Gmail/Calendar OAuth scope review
queued.note: Will hold this until real integration work starts — not yet connected.

agents.name: Steward
agents.title: Financial Tracking & Budgeting
agents.summary: Income, expenses, savings, and investments — tracked, never moved, without approval.
agents.description: Steward owns budgeting, savings goals, and investment awareness. Right now it works entirely on manually entered numbers — no bank or Notion connection yet, by design, until the permission and approval system around money is proven out.
directives: Track only what's entered manually until financial-account integration is approved
Never move money or initiate a transaction — Steward reports, it doesn't act
Route any account-connection request through Sentinel first
Keep category structure consistent with Daniel's existing personal budget format

completed.title: Budget planner UI shipped
completed.note: Income, expense (30 categories matched to Daniel's existing planner), savings goals, investments, and debt payoff calculators, all manual-entry.

completed.title: Formula reference built
completed.note: Documented every calculation as both a live JS formula and its Excel/Sheets equivalent.

inProgress.title: Category reconciliation
inProgress.note: Matching category list 1:1 against Daniel's '2026 Annual Personal Financial Planner' sheet.

queued.title: SQLite persistence
queued.note: Move planner state from in-browser memory to a real backend table once Engineer wires POST /api/steward/budget.

queued.title: Recurring expense scheduler
queued.note: Mirror the sheet's 'Recurring Expenses' tab — auto-apply monthly recurring items.

queued.title: Notion sync (read-only first)
queued.note: Held pending Sentinel review of integration scope.

queued.title: Bank account connection
queued.note: Explicitly deferred — AGENTS.md flags this as needing a much stronger approval system first.

queued.title: Monthly digest
queued.note: Auto-generated month-end summary: budget vs actual, savings progress, net worth delta.

agents.name: Link
agents.title: API & Integration Design
agents.summary: Designs how R.A.M.B.O. talks to the outside world.
agents.description: Link figures out how a new integration should connect — auth flow, scopes, rate limits, failure handling — before Engineer builds it. It does not hold credentials itself.
directives: Design the minimum viable scope for any new integration
Prefer read-only access until a write capability is explicitly needed
Document token refresh and failure behavior up front
Hand credential storage decisions to Keeper, never store secrets in Link's own notes

completed.title: Manual JSON contract
completed.note: Defined the shape of data/manual_context.json so it can later be swapped for a live API response with no schema change.

inProgress.title: Calendar .ics ingestion design
inProgress.note: Mapping a local .ics export to the same internal schema used for live Calendar API later.

queued.title: Gmail/Calendar OAuth scope draft
queued.note: Will design narrowest possible read scopes once Sentinel's approval flow is ready.

agents.name: Keeper
agents.title: Storage, Database & Memory
agents.summary: Owns the notebook — what gets remembered and how.
agents.description: Keeper manages the SQLite memory store: recent runs, Brain journal history, and eventually budget and integration data. It's the layer between 'happened once' and 'R.A.M.B.O. can recall it later.'
directives: Keep the memory schema simple until a real need forces complexity
Never expose raw database access outside the Brain layer
Version any schema change so old data still reads correctly
Coordinate with Sentinel before storing anything sensitive

completed.title: Recent-run memory table
completed.note: Original MVP memory store — saves recent dashboard runs.

inProgress.title: Agent journal schema
inProgress.note: Designing the table that will eventually back every agent's journal page instead of baked-in HTML.

queued.title: Steward budget tables
queued.note: income, expenses, savings_goals, investments, debts — schema draft for SQLite.

queued.title: Approval/audit log table
queued.note: Backing store for Sentinel's audit trail.

queued.title: Data migration plan
queued.note: Path from mock data to real data without breaking the dashboard.

agents.name: Echo
agents.title: Conversation & Communication
agents.description: Echo owns tone and phrasing for anything user-facing — dashboard copy, summaries, future voice responses. Calm command center, sharp operator, plain-spoken. No jargon unless asked for.
directives: Keep language plain — explain like Daniel is smart but not a developer
Match the R.A.M.B.O. personality: calm, confidence-building, not theatrical
Never soften a Sentinel warning to sound friendlier
Hold wake-phrase and voice-response design until Phase 7

completed.title: Council page copy pass
completed.note: Wrote agent descriptions and journal copy in plain, non-jargon language.

inProgress.title: Empty-state copy
inProgress.note: Writing what each agent page says before it has real journal data.

queued.title: Wake-phrase response drafts
queued.note: Pre-writing Echo's spoken responses for Phase 7, even though voice isn't live yet.

agents.name: Pilot
agents.title: Task Management & Follow-Up
agents.summary: Keeps track of what's open, what's done, and what's next.
agents.description: Pilot owns the queue itself — across every Brain. It's the reason 'what's left to do' has one answer instead of eleven different lists.
directives: Maintain one unified view of queued work across all Brains
Flag stalled tasks before they go silent
Surface what's blocking what
Hand status, never priority calls — that's Architect/R.A.M.B.O.

completed.title: Round Table task tracking
completed.note: Logged today's build across Architect, Engineer, Steward, and Echo into one timeline.

inProgress.title: Cross-agent queue view
inProgress.note: Building the unified 'everything queued, everywhere' list for the dashboard.

queued.title: Stalled-task alerts
queued.note: Flag anything queued more than 7 days with no movement.

systemPages.name: Approvals
systemPages.title: Sentinel Approval Queue
systemPages.description: A cross-Brain view of everything waiting on Daniel's sign-off. Anything that spends money, sends a message, deletes a file, or touches a new account lands here before it happens — owned by Sentinel, visible to every Brain.

queue.title: None pending
queue.note: No Brain has a request waiting on approval right now. This view will populate live once Sentinel's audit log (Keeper) is wired in.

recentDecisions.title: Steward manual-entry mode
recentDecisions.decision: Auto-cleared — no money moved, no account connected, no approval required.

systemPages.name: Learning
systemPages.title: R.A.M.B.O. Learning Log
systemPages.description: What R.A.M.B.O. has picked up from running the Brain network so far: patterns, corrections, routing improvements, and process changes.

entries.title: Round Table build
entries.note: Council pages work best generated from one data source instead of hand-authored per agent — keeps every page consistent when content changes.

entries.title: Approval scoping
entries.note: Manual, non-connected data entry (like Steward's planner) doesn't need Sentinel approval. Only live account connections and money movement do.
